key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn the static mechanical response of additively manufactured short-fiber thermoplastics from microstructure topology and history, replacing expensive mesoscale FE runs.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, physics-informed screening of weak microstructural regions in SFT components to support design, calibration, and digital-twin workflows for aerospace and automotive parts.
method: Reconstruct microstructures from micro-CT and discretize them into Voronoi-based fiber-interaction neighborhoods.
Homogenize each cell with nonlinear finite element simulations including matrix damage.
Train a GNN-LSTM to map microstructural graphs and loading history to stress-strain response.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches unseen FE responses with about R^2≈0.98 and reduces computation by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the fidelity of the reconstructed microstructures, calibrated damage laws, and the representativeness of the training set for unseen manufacturing variability.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
